DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mary Kate Kamka, Esq. of Severson & Werson, (415-398-3344), for Wells Fargo Bank, Defendant and moving party.
Eunji Cho, Esq. of Mellen Law Firm, (650-638-0120), for Paymon Ghafouri, Plaintiff and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's Demurrer to Third Amended Complaint is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Plaintiff does not merely allege inducement to default as in Franczak, Plaintiff alleges bad faith in Defendant's exercise of discretion regarding forbearance under DOT 12.See Harvey v. Bank of America 2013 WL 632088; Harris v. Wells Fargo 2013 WL1820003. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Judge: Lynn O. Taylor
Court Clerk: J. Rios Merida
Reporter: None

09:45 AM: Case is called for hearing of demurrer to First Amended Complaint;
No appearance by either side. Tentative ruling stands:

Defendant's Demurer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to (1) first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty to allege facts showing that broker actively concealed her breach; to allege that breach could not have been discovered despite reasonable diligence; and to explain the discrepancies between allegation that loan was "fixed interest" for seven years (FAC 4:23) and that payment jumped in 12 moths (FAC 4:26); (2) second cause of action for quiet title to allege unconditional tender under the note and DOT at issue or to allege facts with particularity regarding fraud rendering DOT void; (3) third cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith a fair dealing to indicate which provision of the contract was violated by assurances that there will be no foreclosure. See FAC paragraph 61. To the extent Plaintiffs base this cause of action on inducement to default, it is sustained without leave to amend. See Franczak v. Suntrust Mortgage 2013 WL 843912. =(611/VJ Taylor)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Megan C. Kelly (415-398-3344) of Severson & Werson for Wells Fargo Bank, defendant and moving party / Sarah Adelaars (650-638-0120) of Mellen Law Firm for Paymon Ghafouri, plaintiff and opposing party

Both parties submit to the tentative ruling as regards the 1st and 2nd causes of action. ARGUED as to the 3rd cause of action. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action: Sustain with leave to amend as to cause of action 1 - Breach of Fiduciary duty to allege; cause of action 2 - Quiet Title to allege tender. Court reserves ruling as to the 3rd cause of action. Defendant is to submit a proposed order in compliance with the tentative ruling as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action. The proposed order as to the 3rd cause of action should state that it is to be either overruled or sustained with leave to amend. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Not reported (D501)
